blocks.name: Lofts y Espacios
blocks.subtitle: Multi-floor Living
blocks.intro: Some apartments have an upper loft level. Place a Loft Bed on the ground floor to unlock the upper space.

blocks.name: Altura y Estructura
blocks.subtitle: Sloped Ceilings & Columns
blocks.intro: Watch the ceiling! Height contour lines show low zones where tall furniture won't fit. Structural columns cannot be moved.

blocks.name: Instalaciones
blocks.subtitle: Plumbing & Electricity
blocks.intro: Use the Subfloor layer to route water pipes and power cables from wall supply points to your furniture.

blocks.name: Desafío Final
blocks.subtitle: Moments & Master Plans
blocks.intro: These apartments have multiple life Moments — the layout must work at different times of day. Use foldable furniture wisely.

tenant.name: Pablo
tenant.bio: Remote worker. Doesn't need much. Still pays on time.
tenant.flavor: Pablo pays 300€/month. Make it barely livable.

tenant.name: Mia
tenant.bio: First-year student. Survives on instant noodles and optimism.
tenant.flavor: Mia needs a place to crash and study. Nothing fancy.

tenant.name: Jonas
tenant.bio: Freelance designer. Needs space for ideas and a decent nap.
tenant.flavor: Jonas wants it creative. Or at least functional.

tenant.name: The Santos
tenant.bio: Young couple. Both work from home. Both cook. Neither cleans.
tenant.flavor: They need the basics. They'll provide the chaos themselves.

tenant.name: Alexandra
tenant.bio: Startup founder. Sleeps 5 hours. Has strong opinions about standing desks.
tenant.flavor: Alexandra wants everything. Obviously.

tenant.name: The Collective
tenant.bio: Three roommates. One WiFi password. Eternal debates about shared chores.
tenant.flavor: They share everything. Except opinions.

mechanic_intro.title: Custom Build
mechanic_intro.body:
This apartment is too cramped for a standard bed.

Select Custom Bed from the CUSTOM BUILD panel on the right, then paint tiles directly onto the floor to shape your own piece. LMB to add tiles, RMB to erase. The shape must be contiguous and not too narrow or elongated.

tenant.name: Lukas
tenant.bio: Intern. Budget: zero. Expectations: also zero.
tenant.flavor: Tiny space. Tiny rent. Tiny soul.

tenant.name: Fatima
tenant.bio: Full-stack developer. Needs a monitor stand AND a bed. Non-negotiable.
tenant.flavor: She bills 80€/hour but rents for 450. Do the math.

tenant.name: The Studio Gang
tenant.bio: Three freelancers sharing a work space. Nobody sleeps here. Allegedly.
tenant.flavor: No beds required. They have homes. Somewhere.

tenant.name: Kenji
tenant.bio: Musician by night, barista by morning. Needs a bed AND a desk.
tenant.flavor: He asks if the walls are soundproofed. They are not.

mechanic_intro.title: New Mechanic: Loft Floors
mechanic_intro.body: Place a Loft Bed (high_bed) on the ground floor to unlock the upper level. Loft spaces let you double your usable area — great for sleep or storage above the main living zone.

tenant.name: Dario
tenant.bio: Aspiring musician. Needs a sleep nook above and a spot to sit below.
tenant.flavor: Small space, big dreams. The loft is the dream.

tenant.name: Ingrid
tenant.bio: Yoga teacher. Needs storage for equipment and peace for the mind.
tenant.flavor: She will leave a very long review on the platform.

tenant.name: Omar & Priya
tenant.bio: Two architects. Will judge your layout professionally.
tenant.flavor: They know exactly how many square metres they're getting.

tenant.name: Elena & Marco
tenant.bio: Just moved in together. Budget is tight. Love is cheaper.
tenant.flavor: They'll argue about the sofa placement. At least it's not your problem.

tenant.name: Dr. Reeves
tenant.bio: Consultant. Holds video calls in 4 time zones simultaneously.
tenant.flavor: He will call the property manager. But only once, and politely.

tenant.name: Sofia
tenant.bio: Art dealer. Knows what she wants. Won't say it twice.
tenant.flavor: She once rejected a Mies van der Rohe chair. Tread carefully.

tenant.name: The Artists
tenant.bio: Five creatives. One WiFi. A shared vision and no shared dishes.
tenant.flavor: Chaotic, beautiful, underpaying. Just like the art world.

tenant.name: The Hoffmanns
tenant.bio: Retired civil servants. Meticulous. Will photograph every scratch.
tenant.flavor: They have their own Tupperware. They will not share it.

tenant.name: Dev & Rina
tenant.bio: Software engineers. Want two separate sleep zones but are too polite to say it.
tenant.flavor: They communicate exclusively via Notion. This listing is in Notion.

tenant.name: Bao & Co.
tenant.bio: Small creative agency. Long space. Short deadlines.
tenant.flavor: They need to fit twelve monitors. This is not a joke.

tenant.name: Herr Kessler
tenant.bio: Retired banker. Standards as high as his floor area.
tenant.flavor: He will not sign the lease if the kitchen is smaller than his ego.

tenant.name: Miriam
tenant.bio: Interior designer. Hates arbitrary walls. Will say so, professionally.
tenant.flavor: She knows that wall is not load-bearing. Do you?

tenant.name: Theo
tenant.bio: Illustrator. Loves slanted light and low ceilings. Mostly.
tenant.flavor: He says the slant gives it character. You still can't fit a wardrobe under it.

tenant.name: The Brauns
tenant.bio: A family of four. They navigate around columns without thinking about it.
tenant.flavor: They want the columns kept. Apparently it is a style choice.

tenant.name: Nadia
tenant.bio: Travels 3 weeks a month. Wants a sofa-bed. No exceptions.
tenant.flavor: She has a suitcase under her arm. The sofa-bed is non-negotiable.

mechanic_intro.title: New Mechanic: Sloped Ceilings
mechanic_intro.body: The height contour lines show ceiling height across the floor. Tall furniture cannot be placed in low-ceiling zones. Plan carefully — only low and medium-height items fit under the slope.

tenant.name: Vera
tenant.bio: Yoga teacher. Loves the slanted light. Hates stubbing her head.
tenant.flavor: Put the desk where she can stand up straight.

tenant.name: The Factory Collective
tenant.bio: Eight people sharing one industrial space. One bathroom. No drama (yet).
tenant.flavor: There are load-bearing columns everywhere. Build around them.

tenant.name: Prof. Schulze
tenant.bio: Art history professor. Wants high ceilings metaphorically. Accepts the slope.
tenant.flavor: The low end is where the bathroom goes. She said so.

tenant.name: The Vogel Family
tenant.bio: Two parents, three kids, and a dog. They need space. All of it.
tenant.flavor: Three floors. Each child gets one. The dog negotiates.

tenant.name: Herr & Frau Weinstein
tenant.bio: Empty nesters. Ready to reclaim their space. Ready to demolish everything.
tenant.flavor: You have a sledgehammer budget and a weekend. Make it open plan.

mechanic_intro.title: New Mechanic: Plumbing & Electricity
mechanic_intro.body: Switch to the Subfloor layer to draw pipe and cable routes. Connect the wall supply points to your furniture. Kitchen units need water; desks and appliances need power.

tenant.name: Tomás
tenant.bio: Chef by day, coder by night. Needs a kitchen and a workstation. Both properly wired.
tenant.flavor: He will notice if the water doesn't reach the sink.

tenant.name: Lena
tenant.bio: UX designer. Wants every appliance connected, every wire hidden.
tenant.flavor: Works from home. Hosts dinner. Sleeps late. Same room, different life.

tenant.name: The Yilmaz-Schmidts
tenant.bio: Blended family of six. They have a cat, a van, and a 3D printer.
tenant.flavor: The cat gets the loft. Obviously.

tenant.name: Countess von Bauer
tenant.bio: Old money. High standards. Sends handwritten complaints on crested paper.
tenant.flavor: She requires the piano area to be unobstructed. There is no piano yet.

tenant.name: Retired General Kroll
tenant.bio: Forty years of service. Walls are for structure, not aesthetics.
tenant.flavor: He respects load-bearing walls. He respects very little else.

mechanic_intro.title: New Mechanic: Day-Night Moments
mechanic_intro.body: Some tenants live differently at different times of day. Each Moment has its own list of needs. Foldable furniture — like a sofa-bed — can cover multiple needs depending on which mode is active.

tenant.name: Sven
tenant.bio: Works from home, cooks twice a day. Same room, two modes.
tenant.flavor: By day a developer, by night just hungry.

tenant.name: The Richters
tenant.bio: Banker + architect. They each have very different opinions about what good taste means.
tenant.flavor: One of them will love it. The other will commission a renovation.

tenant.name: Ariadne Wolf
tenant.bio: Gallerist. Has exquisite taste and an exquisite budget to match.
tenant.flavor: Works from the apartment, hosts openings, and sleeps in the loft. All three must be perfect.

tenant.name: The Stadtpalast Collective
tenant.bio: A tech CEO, a sculptor, and their twelve mutual friends. This is the end game.
tenant.flavor: Three floors. Twelve friends. One vision. The space must work at 8am, 8pm, and midnight.
